text: When you write code, bugs are inevitable. Identifying bugs is the first step—look for unexpected behavior or error messages. A systematic approach helps you fix them efficiently. Start by isolating the problem: test small sections of code to find where things go wrong. Debugging tools like breakpoints let you pause execution and inspect variables step by step. Logging is another powerful technique—by printing messages to the console, you can track what your program is doing in real time. Once you understand the bug, test your fix carefully. With practice, debugging becomes a logical process, not a frustrating one.
